perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). [***]
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). Heritage is encouraged but not required to meet the production minimums outlined on Schedule A. [***]
perturbation.explanation: By adding that Heritage is encouraged, but not required, to meet production minimums directly contradicts the initial statement that they 'shall produce' according to Schedule A, making adherence to Schedule A optional. This weakens the contract and undermines the original intent of fixed production obligations. The contradiction arises when considering Section 2(a) in the context of overall agreement enforcement, where it would be expected that 'shall produce' imposes a binding obligation
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2(a) of the Agreement is amended so that the first sentence that previously read: "Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). Heritage is encouraged but not required to meet the production minimums outlined on Schedule A. [***]" Source: BELLRING BRANDS, INC., S-1, 9/20/2019 now reads: "Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A and Schedule A-1 attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products," each individual unit of Product "Unit"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). [***], except that, notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, [***]."
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that Heritage is 'encouraged but not required' to meet production minimums in Schedule A. This directly contradicts the original obligation in Section 2(a) that 'Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A'. The addition undermines the mandatory nature of the production outlined in Schedule A, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding whether Heritage is legally bound to meet those minimums. Section 2(a) initially establishes a definite obligation, but the modification introduces optionality, making the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall purchase all ingredients and packaging materials identified in Schedule C to be used in connection with the manufacturer of the Products. Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing as identified on Schedule C.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage may purchase some or all of the ingredients and packaging materials identified in Schedule C to be used in connection with the manufacturer of the Products. Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing as identified on Schedule C.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall purchase all' to 'may purchase some or all' creates ambiguity about Heritage's obligations regarding purchasing ingredients. This directly contradicts the expectation that Heritage is responsible for all ingredient procurement as defined in Schedule C, introducing uncertainty. The contradiction will become apparent in Section 3(b), where a party could argue that Heritage is not obligated to procure all specified materials, potentially disrupting production
perturbation.location: 4. Section 3 DELIVER, PRICING, BILLING AND PAYMENT a. Section 3(b)
perturbation.contradicted_location: 4. Section 3 DELIVER, PRICING, BILLING AND PAYMENT a. Section 3(b)
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3(b) of the Agreement is amended so that whereas it previously read: "Heritage shall purchase all ingredients and packaging materials identified in Schedule C to be used in connection with the manufacturer of the Products. Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing as identified on Schedule C." now reads: "Heritage may purchase some or all of the ingredients and packaging materials identified in the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-l to be used in connection with the manufacturer of the Products. Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing as identified on the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-l. Heritage shall not, however, purchase ingredients or packaging materials in excess of those required [***]."
perturbation.justification: The original text, 'Heritage shall purchase all ingredients and packaging materials identified in Schedule C,' created a clear obligation. The changed text, 'Heritage may purchase some or all of the ingredients and packaging materials identified in Schedule C,' introduces ambiguity by making the purchase discretionary. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (which is essentially the same clause being modified), creating an in-text contradiction regarding Heritage's responsibility for procuring all necessary materials outlined in Schedule C, per the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered to Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A, which Schedule may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage.
perturbation.changed_text: All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered to Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall generally conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A, which Schedule may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in most respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall conform' to 'shall generally conform' and 'in all material respects' to 'in most respects' introduces a significant ambiguity regarding the exact standards the Products need to meet. This weakens the specifications adherence requirement. Section 2(o)(i), which details product specifications, becomes undermined. It's now unclear the precise level of conformity expected, leading to potential disputes over whether products meet the agreement's terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2(o)(i) of the Agreement is amended so that the first sentence that previously read: "All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered to Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A, which Schedule may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage." now reads: "All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall generally conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1, which Schedules may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in most respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage."
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the standard of conformity for the products, replacing 'shall conform' with 'shall generally conform' and 'in all material respects' with 'in most respects'. This introduces ambiguity and directly conflicts with the original text which stated a stricter requirement for conformity. The Contradicted Text is in the same location. The changed language creates a lower standard and undermines the original, more definite, specification, thus creating a contradiction.
